etymology.description: In the word 地图(地圖) (dìtú, "map"), 地(地) (dì, "earth; ground") refers to the land or ground, and 圖(圖) (tú, "diagram; picture") refers to a drawing or diagram. Together, these characters are used to describe a diagram or representation of a section of the earth's surface or ground, which is essentially what a map is.
etymology.interpretation: A 地圖(地图) (dìtú, "map") is a representation or diagram of the earth's surface, which fits logically with its components: 地(地) (dì, "earth") and 圖(圖) (tú, "diagram").

examples.chinese: 他给我看了一张地图(他給我看了一張地圖)。
examples.pinyin: Tā gěi wǒ kàn le yī zhāng dìtú.

examples.chinese: 我在寻找图书馆的位置(我在尋找圖書館的位置)，但地图不是很清晰(但地圖不是很清晰)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zài xúnzhǎo túshūguǎn de wèizhì, dàn dìtú bù shì hěn qīngxī.
examples.english: I am looking for the library's location, but the map is not very clear.

examples.chinese: 这本书包含了世界各地的地图(這本書包含了世界各地的地圖)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè běn shū bāohán le shìjiè gèdì de dìtú.
examples.english: This book contains maps of various places around the world.
